title: Keyboard-Navigable Dropdown
description:
Build an **accessible dropdown menu** that supports full keyboard navigation following WAI-ARIA menu pattern.

### Requirements

- Trigger button toggles the menu open/closed
- Trigger uses `aria-haspopup="true"` and `aria-expanded`
- Menu has `role="menu"` and each item has `role="menuitem"`
- **Arrow Down** moves focus to the next menu item
- **Arrow Up** moves focus to the previous menu item
- **Home** key moves focus to the first item
- **End** key moves focus to the last item
- **Enter** or **Space** activates the focused menu item
- **Escape** closes the menu and returns focus to the trigger
- Clicking outside the menu closes it
- When menu opens, focus moves to the first menu item

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Trigger button | `dropdown-trigger` |
| Menu container | `dropdown-menu` |
| Menu items | `menu-item-{index}` |
| Output area | `output` |
constraints:
- Must follow WAI-ARIA menu design pattern
- Arrow keys must not scroll the page (use preventDefault)
- Focus management must be explicit (roving tabindex or aria-activedescendant)
- Must handle clicks outside to close
hints: Use a roving tabindex pattern: the focused menu item has tabIndex={0}, all others have tabIndex={-1}. Call .focus() when arrow keys change the active index.
Store a ref to an array of menu item elements. On arrow key, compute the next index with modular arithmetic and focus that element.
Use useEffect with a mousedown listener on document to detect outside clicks and close the menu.
